MINI MINUTES FOR SEP-26-2013 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT'S DEMURRER TO CIBA PLAINTIFFS' FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED. AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION (NEGLIGENCE AGAINST ALL DEFENDANTS), THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. COMMON LAW NEGLIGENCE IS NOT A PERMISSIBLE CAUSE OF ACTION AGAINST A PUBLIC ENTITY. (TOLAN V. STATE OF CALIFORNIA EX REL. DEP'T OF TRANSPORTATION (1979) 100 CAL.APP.3D 980, 986.) AS TO ALL OTHER CAUSES OF ACTION, THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PLED WITH PARTICULARITY EVERY FACT ESSENTIAL TO THE EXISTENCE OF STATUTORY LIABILITY. (SUSMAN V. CITY OF LOS ANGELES (1969) 269 CAL.APP.2D 803, 809; SEE ALSO MITTENHUBER V. CITY OF REDONDO BEACH (1983) 142 CAL.APP.3D 1, 5, "BECAUSE RECOVERY IS BASED ON A STATUTORY CAUSE OF ACTION, THE PLAINTIFF MUST SET FORTH FACTS IN HIS COMPLAINT SUFFICIENTLY DETAILED AND SPECIFIC TO SUPPORT AN INFERENCE THAT EACH OF THE STATUTORY ELEMENTS OF LIABILITY IS SATISFIED. GENERAL ALLEGATIONS ARE REGARDED AS INADEQUATE.") THE COURT FINDS THAT THE COMPLAINT ADEQUATELY PLEADS COMPLIANCE WITH THE PRESENTATION REQUIREMENT OF THE GOVERNMENT CLAIMS ACT. (SEE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, 5.) THE COURT DID NOT CONSIDER THE DECLARATION OF JOHN C. ELLIS, AS IT WAS NOT ATTACHED TO THE FAC AS AN EXHIBIT, BUT WAS A SEPARATELY FILED DOCUMENT, AND THEREFORE, THE FACTS ALLEGED THEREIN MAY NOT BE CONSIDERED ON DEMURRER. (SEE BLANK V. KIRWAN (1985) 39 CAL.3D 311, 318, HOLDING THAT A DEMURRER ONLY CONSIDERS ALL MATERIAL FACTS PROPERLY PLEADED IN THE COMPLAINT AND MATTERS WHICH MAY BE JUDICIALLY NOTICED.) (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: LESLIE C. NICHOLS, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-22-2013 Continued To Jan-08-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-26-2013 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Defendant San Francisco Unified School District's Demurrer to CIBA Plaintiffs' First Amended Complaint is sustained. As to the fourth cause of action (Negligence Against All Defendants), the demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. Common law negligence is not a permissible cause of action against a public entity. (Tolan v. State of California ex rel. Dep't of Transportation (1979) 100 Cal.App.3d 980, 986.) As to all other causes of action, the demurrer is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. Plaintiff has not pled with particularity every fact essential to the existence of statutory liability. (Susman v. City of Los Angeles (1969) 269 Cal.App.2d 803, 809; see also Mittenhuber v. City of Redondo Beach (1983) 142 Cal.App.3d 1, 5, "Because recovery is based on a statutory cause of action, the plaintiff must set forth facts in his complaint sufficiently detailed and specific to support an inference that each of the statutory elements of liability is satisfied. General allegations are regarded as inadequate.") The Court finds that the Complaint adequately pleads compliance with the presentation requirement of the Government Claims Act. (See First Amended Complaint, 5.) The Court did not consider the Declaration of John C. Ellis, as it was not attached to the FAC as an Exhibit, but was a separately filed document, and therefore, the facts alleged therein may not be considered on demurrer. (See Blank v. Kirwan (1985) 39 Cal.3d 311, 318, holding that a demurrer only considers all material facts properly pleaded in the complaint and matters which may be judicially noticed.) (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Leslie C. Nichols, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185=(302/LCN)
